name: Tree Houseleek
likes: Lots of sun, very little water
introduction: This plant stores water in its thick leaves, so it can survive long dry periods.
fun_fact: It can grow on rocks and walls, almost like it doesn't need soil!
hint: Look for a sunny, dry area with rocks or walls. It might be growing where other plants can't!

name: Dragon Tree
introduction: A strange tree with an umbrella shape that helps it survive in harsh climates.
fun_fact: If you cut it, red sap comes out that looks like dragon's blood!
hint: Search in the dry, desert-like area for a tree shaped like a giant umbrella.

name: Arum Lily
introduction: A beautiful plant with large white flowers that love damp environments.
fun_fact: Its flower isn't just one petal, it's actually a curled leaf!
hint: Look in a cool, shady and slightly wet area — maybe near water or mist.

name: Blue Gum
introduction: A fast-growing tree known for its strong smell and smooth bark.
fun_fact: Koalas love eating eucalyptus leaves, even though they're poisonous to most animals!
hint: Try to smell your way to it! Look for a tall tree in a sunny area with a fresh scent.

name: Pebble Plants
likes: Very little water and lots of sun
introduction: These tiny plants look exactly like stones to avoid being eaten.
fun_fact: They can "disappear" in the desert because they blend in so well!
hint: Look closely at the ground in a dry area — are those stones… or plants?

name: Donkey's Tail
introduction: A hanging plant with long stems full of thick, juicy leaves.
fun_fact: Its leaves fall off super easily, even a small touch can make them drop!
hint: Look up! This plant likes to hang down from pots or ledges.

name: Angel's Trumpet
likes: Warmth, sun, and lots of water
introduction: A plant with big, hanging flowers shaped like trumpets.
fun_fact: It smells amazing at night to attract insects, but it's actually poisonous!
hint: Look for big, hanging flowers — like trumpets pointing down from a bush or tree.

name: Indian Clock Vine
likes: Warm, humid air and support to climb on
introduction: A climbing plant with colorful, tube-shaped flowers.
fun_fact: Its flowers twist as they grow, like they're slowly turning to "look around"!
hint: Search for a climbing plant wrapping around something — maybe a wall or a pole.

name: Peladera
likes: Warmth, humidity, and lots of water
introduction: A tropical plant that grows fruit similar to papaya.
fun_fact: Its fruit grows straight out of the trunk, not the branches, almost like it's glued on!
hint: Look for a tree where fruits grow directly on the trunk — not on branches!

name: Giant Water Lilies
likes: Warm water and lots of sunlight
introduction: Huge floating plants with giant round leaves.
fun_fact: Their leaves are so strong they can hold small animals, even a child! But please don’t try it out ;)
hint: Find a pond or water area and look for giant round leaves floating on the surface.
